_README: Optional. Every value here is the documented default — delete this file and
the skill behaves identically. Edit a value to override it.
Paths without a leading / or ~ are relative to the vault root.
Environment variables win over this file: $PKM (vault root),

_trusted_local_text: Set to a directory or file holding an authorised complete Vachanamrut
text and the skill will prefer it over any network fetch. Expected shape:
one file per discourse named by canonical ref (e.g. 'Gadhada I-15.md'),
or a single file with '# <ref>' headings. See README 'Adding a trusted

_min_confidence_to_suggest_ref: Floor for naming a discourse number at all: 'tentative' | 'moderate' |
'high'. Raise it to make the skill stay silent about references it cannot
nearly confirm. Below the floor it reports 'unresolved'.
_unresolved_link_policy: 'plain-text' names a missing note without brackets (default; creates no
stubs). 'stub-link' would emit [[...]] for notes that do not exist —
only enable if you deliberately want unresolved links in the graph.
_update_vachanamrut_backlinks: Even when true, a backlink is written only if the connection is
verified, the note exists, the link is absent, and it already has a
'## Links' section. MOCs are never touched.
_respect_qc_lock: Take the flock shared with obsidian-capture and qc-process before
writing to a QC note. Turning this off risks corrupting a capture
session or a nightly Hermes run. Leave it on.
